title: Ort Hoogra-D'En

'''Ort Hoogra-D'En''' was an infamous [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] terrorist wanted by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Despite his anti-Empire feelings, he had no interest in joining the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

Born on [[Moltok/Legends|Moltok]], Hoogra-D'En grew up with a keen interest in [[Plant/Legends|plants]]. He eventually moved to the Ho'Din colony [[Anemcoro]] to study the vast exotic plant life there. He was forced to depart Anemcoro following the near-complete destruction of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces involved in a dispute between two neighboring planets, eighteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].

Hoogra-D'En swore vengeance for the loss of so much exotic plant life on his adopted home and began to prepare to unleash his fury on the Empire, specifically Imperial interests in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].

Over the next few years, Hoogra-D'En relocated his operations to the planet [[Pallaxides]] and built up a network of allies, including a male [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Tergeth Dilorr]], a [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] named [[Babel Senjorg]], and several [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] who made up the bulk of his forces. On Pallaxides, Hoogra-D'En was able to cultivate samples of the plant life he rescued from Anemcoro and used these in his operations.

[[Tamack]] plants were the most important component to the creation of Hoogra-D'En's [[spore]] [[Bomb/Legends|bombs]]. Tamack spores were carefully harvested periodically from the plants and placed inside a metal container. Once activated, the spore bombs released the microscopic spores into the air and began infecting anyone in the immediate area. The spores stayed active for three [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]], an improvement from the spore's normal thirty-minute lifespan. Nothing less than full [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] immersion could cure an individual of the [[Tamack spore plague]]. Hoogra-D'En also used a [[Water/Legends|water]] plant called [[Fahla]] to hide his base on Pallaxides from any [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] scans.

Eventually, Hoogra-D'En's attacks in the Outer Rim became too bold when he [[Bombing of Syndaar Military Prep School|unleashed]] a spore bomb on the [[Syndaar Military Prep School]] on [[Syndaar]]. The father of one of his victims, [[System Sub-Commander]] [[Jared Brojtal]], organized all of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] forces available to him and began a massive manhunt for the terrorist. Eventually, Hoogra-D'En's base on Pallaxides was located by Brojtal's forces and [[Attack on Ort Hoogra-D'En's base|bombarded]].
